refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support teleoperation with communication quality that meets a defined packet loss limit. It is focused on what the system must do (support teleoperation under a communication quality constraint) without specifying how to achieve it, thus maintaining solution neutrality. There are no multiple obligations joined by 'and' or 'or' that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control system
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality management within the protocol layer, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be designed modularly to meet packet loss requirements without tightly coupling to other system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that adaptive mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes error correction and retransmission can be tuned to meet packet loss without violating other performance constraints
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency and jitter constraints in the FR to ensure teleoperation quality beyond packet loss, enabling more comprehensive DP selection.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol implementations responsible for managing data transmission, including error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving packets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send status and performance data related to communication protocol operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as error correction and retransmission are core to network communication.
Vehicle module may use communication but does not implement protocol details; high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol implementation here would cause diffusion to other modules.
Safety Services may monitor communication but should not implement protocol; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication functions and can encapsulate adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If protocol parameters need to be adjusted by other modules, interface design must prevent diffusion of protocol logic.
Integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services requires well-defined interfaces to avoid coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, thus fulfilling the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If protocol parameters need to be adjusted by other modules, interface design must prevent diffusion of protocol logic.
Integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services requires well-defined interfaces to avoid coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating that the DP is well contained within this IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module. The communication protocol logic is centralized here, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and testing under agreed network conditions to ensure packet loss ≤ 1%.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the communication protocol DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces defined for network link and telemetry, supporting clear ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interfaces and access controls to prevent other modules from implementing or modifying protocol logic, reducing diffusion risk.
Ensure that dependencies on lower-level hardware interfaces are fully encapsulated or abstracted within the NGHE Solution module.
Establish well-defined integration interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules to avoid tight coupling and maintain modularity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear justification, high containment, low diffusion risk, and feasible verification. The recommended revisions are minor interface and encapsulation improvements that do not require further decomposition of the functional requirements. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-contained implementation module is crucial, and the current level meets these criteria effectively.

revise.why: To define clear interfaces and access controls preventing other modules from modifying protocol logic, encapsulate hardware dependencies fully, and establish well-defined integration interfaces to avoid tight coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate all necessary hardware interfaces.
Telemetry interface provides reliable packet loss monitoring.
Integration interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services can be clearly defined and maintained.
open_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated.
Risk of protocol logic diffusion if interfaces are not strictly controlled.
Integration coupling with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Verification test results showing packet loss ≤ 1% under agreed network conditions.
Interface definitions and access control policies preventing unauthorized protocol logic changes.
Documentation of encapsulation of hardware dependencies within NGHE Solution module.
